channel.title: The Cambridge Record — homelessness
channel.description: Everything on the record about homelessness.

item.title: POR 2026-121 — That the City Manager is requested to use the Federal Stabilization Fund to provide one-time stabilization payments to C
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to use the Federal Stabilization Fund to provide one-time stabilization payments to Cambridge-based and Cambridge-serving organizations impacted by FY27 MOVA cuts, report back to the City Council with a proposed funding plan, and work with the Mayor's Office and ot

item.title: CMA 2026-154 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a cross statutory transfer of $322,825.00 within
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a cross statutory transfer of $322,825.00 within the School Department General Fund.

item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a response to Awaiting Report Item Number 26-23 regarding the Cambridge Preschool Program.

item.description: That the City Manager is requested to begin discussions with MIT, CASPAR, the co-chairs of the Human Services and Veterans Committee, all relevant city departments and the shelter residents to develop a plan to renovate and expand 240 Albany Street in order to provide additional low threshold shelte

item.title: RES 2025 #229 — City Council thanks to the Sufi Service Committee (Boston) and its volunteers for a decade of selfless service to our mo
item.description: City Council thanks to the Sufi Service Committee (Boston) and its volunteers for a decade of selfless service to our most vulnerable neighbors, and in recognizing and the Committee for their vital contributions to addressing food insecurity, homelessness, and poverty in Cambridge

item.description: Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $275,237.00 to the Grant Fund Human Services Other Ordinary Maintenance account. item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-27, regarding the City Manager investigate bike pod storage options to be placed in suitable areas in the city to provide residents and visitors safe storage options

item.title: POR 2025 #108 — That the City Manager is requested to coordinate with the Community Safety Department, the Cambridge Police Department,
item.description: That the City Manager is requested to coordinate with the Community Safety Department, the Cambridge Police Department, and other relevant parties to ensure that the overnight use of the garden area between St. James Episcopal Church and the Beech Street condos is actively monitored

item.title: CMA 2025 #133 — A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a proposed increase in the FY26 budget of $1 mil
item.description: A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a proposed increase in the FY26 budget of $1 million that would create new municipal vouchers and supportive services for people who are unhoused and a $5 million free cash appropriation for a Federal Grant Stabilization Fund

item.description: That the City Manager is requested to work with all appropriate Departments to develop language to create an “Affordable Rent Incentive Program” program for Cambridge and to report back to the City Council with option, including the percentage of possible tax abatements

item.description: City Council support of a fair union contract for Cambridge Hospital residents and calls on CHA leadership to swiftly and thoughtfully settle a contract that reflects the value of the resident physicians, the patients they serve, and the crucial importance of Cambridge Hospital to our community

item.description: That the Cambridge City Council go on record supporting HD.3095 and SD.2155: An Act Improving Emergency Housing Assistance for Children and Families Experiencing Homelessness

item.title: — COM 120 #2021 : A communication was received from Carolyn Magid, regarding PO 2021 #36: Task Force Transparency PO 2021
item.description: COM 120 #2021 : A communication was received from Carolyn Magid, regarding PO 2021 #36: Task Force Transparency PO 2021#38: Shelter Wages.

item.title: AR-21-9 — Report on providing an overview of various programs and services that are designed to assist the City’s chronically unho
item.description: Report on providing an overview of various programs and services that are designed to assist the City’s chronically unhoused population and those in danger of becoming unhoused, along with the metrics by which the City determines the effectiveness of these programs

item.title: POR 2021 #38 — Shelter Wages CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR ZONDERVAN IN COUNCIL MARCH 1, 2021
item.description: Shelter Wages CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR ZONDERVAN IN COUNCIL MARCH 1, 2021

item.title: POR 2021 #30 — Policy Order re Services for the Unhoused
item.description: Policy Order re Services for the Unhoused

item.title: POR 2021 #12 — That the city manager be and hereby is requested short term measures to fund, support unhoused members of the community
item.description: That the city manager be and hereby is requested short term measures to fund, support unhoused members of the community including public showers, portable toilets, hand washing stations and food programs until further notice

item.title: POR 2021 #13 — That the City further investigate renting space from the New England school of English to house appropriate members of o
item.description: That the City further investigate renting space from the New England school of English to house appropriate members of our unhoused community. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL JANUARY 11, 2021

item.title: CMA 2020 #278 — Transmitting Communication from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of new Emergency Soluti
item.description: Transmitting Communication from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of new Emergency Solutions Grant (ESG) funds from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) in the amount of $2,268.869.00 to the Grant Fund Department of Human Services Programs Salary

item.title: — COF 2020 #66 : A communication was received from Dr. Kenneth N. Salim, Ed. D. Superintendent of Schools, transmitting a
item.description: COF 2020 #66 : A communication was received from Dr. Kenneth N. Salim, Ed. D. Superintendent of Schools, transmitting a presentation regarding Reopening Schools.

item.description: Transmitting Communication from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of new Emergency Solutions Grant (ESG) funds from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) in the amount of $551,448 to the Grant Fund Human Services Salary and Wages account ($57,294)

item.description: Transmitting Communication from Louis A. item.description: Transmitting Communication from Louis A. DePasquale, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of the Massachusetts Housing and Shelter Alliance grant funded by the Department of Housing and Community Development (DHCD) in the amount of $112,119.46 to the Grant Fund Human Service Programs Salary a
